species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 423 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 138; Larsen, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 16 (2002) 9.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, China, Continental Asia, Europe, India, Japan, Korea, Middle East, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, W Asia. LUZON: Benguet, MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos).
species.notes: On wet cliffs, open seepage slopes, etc. According to Merrill (1923), apparently indigenous in Benguet. 1300-2400m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 19 January 2020

species.citation: Baumg. , Enum. Stirp. Transsilv. 1 (1816) 425 ; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 10. ssp. vulgare (Hartm.) Greuter & Burdet, Willdenowia 12 (1982) 37; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 10; ≡ Cerastium vulgare Hartm., Handb. Skand. Fl. (1820) 182. Distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, China, Continental Asia, Europe, Java, Korea, Middle East, New Guinea, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Tibet. Notes: 2400-3000m. Native.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, China, Continental Asia, Europe, Java, Korea, Middle East, New Guinea, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Tibet.

species.citation: Thuill. , Fl. Env. Paris, 2nd Ed. (1799) 226 ; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 11. = Cerastium vulgatum L. , Fl. Suec. (Linnaeus), 2nd Ed. (1755) 158, nom. inval. ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 138.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, China, Europe, India, Java, Malay Peninsula, Middle East, Nepal, New Guinea, Pakistan, Philippines, Tibet, Vietnam, W Asia. LUZON: Benguet.
China, Continental Asia, India, Korea, Nepal, Vietnam.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) Willd. ex Roem. & Schult. , Syst. Veg., 15th Ed. 5 (1819) 406 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 139; ≡ Holosteum cordatum L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 88. = Drymaria cordata var. diandra (Sw.) Griseb. , Fl. Brit. W.I. (1859) 56 ; Duke, Ann. Miss. Bot. Gard. 48 (1961) 253; Hatusima, Mem. Fac. Agr. Kagoshima Univ. 5 (1966) 27; ≡ Holosteum diandrum Sw. , Prodr. (Swartz) (1788) 27.
species.notes: Widespread in the Philippines. Very common weed in rice paddies, open damp slopes, damp thickets, abaca plantations, etc., from the lowlands to 2000m.

genera.last_edited: Edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 24 September 2013
Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 8 July 2022

species.citation: (L.) Lam. , Tabl. Encycl. 2 (1797) 129 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 139; Bakker, Acta Botanica Neerlandica 6 (1957) 51; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 28; ≡ Achyranthes corymbosa L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 205; Type: from Sri Lanka.
species.distribution: Pantropic. LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Mountain Province, Tarlac, Zambales, MINDANAO: Cotabato, South Cotabato.
species.notes: In open, dry, sandy places, dry slopes, etc. Locally abundant. 0-1500m.

species.citation: (Forssk.) Decne. , Ann. Sci. Nat. (Paris), 2nd Sér. 3 (1835) 263; ≡ Polycarpon prostratum (Forssk.) Asch. & Schweinf. , Oesterr. Bot. Z. 39 (1889) 128 ; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 31; ≡ Alsine prostrata Forssk. , Fl. Aegypt.-Arab. (1775) 207; Type: from Egypt. = Polycarpon indicum (Retz.) Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 302 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 139; ≡ Loeflingia indica Retz. , Observ. Bot. (Retzius) 4 (1786) 8. = Polycarpon loeflingiae (Wall. ex Wight & Arn.) Benth. & Hook.f. , Gen. Pl. (Bentham & Hooker f.) 1 (1862) 153; ≡ Hapalosia loeflingiae Wall. ex Wight & Arn. , Prodr. Fl. Ind. Orient. 1 (1834) 358. = Polycarpon polyphyllum Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 53.
species.distribution: Africa, Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Laos, Middle East, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Thailand. LUZON: Pampanga.
species.notes: Known from Arayat (Merrill 1462, US). Apparently very rare and local in the Philippines.

genera.last_edited: Edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 24 September 2013

species.citation: Warb. , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 16 (1892) 14 ; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 35; Type: from New Guinea.
species.distribution: Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Nueva Vizcaya.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 128 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 138; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 36.
species.distribution: Africa, Continental Asia, Europe, Middle East, W Asia. LUZON: Benguet.
species.notes: Naturalized on wet cliffs, open seepage slopes, etc. 1300-2700m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 440 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 139.
species.notes: Native of Europe, now a cosmopolitan weed in the N temperate zone and montane parts of the tropics such as Java, Philippines and New Guinea. A thoroughly naturalized and very common weed in temperate vegetable farms, 1400-2400m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 22 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) Vill. , Hist. Pl. Dauphiné (Villars) 3 (1789) 615 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 138; ≡ Alsine media L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 272.
species.distribution: Africa, Continental Asia, Europe, Middle East, W Asia. LUZON: Benguet.
species.notes: An introduced weed in the Philippines. Waste places, vegetable farms, etc. 1500-2300m.

species.citation: Kurz , J. Bot. 11 (1873) 194 ; Larsen, Fl. Males., ser. 1, 16 (2002) 49; Type: from China. = Stellaria laxa Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 12 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 138; Type: Elmer 6612 (NY, PNH), Luzon.
